core_mechanism: sensitivity-calibrated additive-noise randomization that caps a Rényi (concentrated) divergence between the output distributions of two inputs differing by a bounded perturbation, with the divergence budget forced to decay as the inverse square of the perturbation size and a matching information-theoretic converse fixing that scaling as optimal
brief_reason: The method builds a shifted-log Gaussian mechanism whose noise certifies a budget v(n)=O(n^{-2}) bounding both Rényi-divergence directions between add-or-remove-one neighbors, then proves a matching Ω(n^{-2}) converse and constant bounds via a many-count information argument, so noise calibration to bound a divergence plus a matching lower bound is the transferable machinery.
